name: Txurregi and Gaztelu Loop from Ollo
name_original: Txurregi desde Ilzarbe – circular por Gaztelu
description: Hike the difficult 6.6-mile Txurregi from Ilzarbe – circular via Gaztelu trail for panoramic views and a challenging ascent.
region_intro: Hiking around Ollo, located in the Valle de Ollo in Navarra, Spain, offers diverse terrain characterized by valleys, natural springs, and mountainous areas. The region features trails leading to landmarks such as the Arteta Salt Flats and the NacederoArteta spring. Hikers can explore paths that ascend to peaks like Txurregi, providing varied elevation and natural features. Best hiking trails around Ollo The most popular hiking route is Cascada de Artazul via the Arteta Salt Flats - circular from Ultzurrun, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete, passing through the notable Arteta Salt Flats. Another top favourite among local hikers is Txurregi and Gaztelu Loop from Ollo, a difficult 6.6 miles (10.6 km) path that explores mountainous terrain and offers summit views. Local hikers also love the NacederoArteta – Arteta Spring Viewpoint loop from Centro de Información y Educación Ambiental, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through a natural spring area, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Ollo is defined by natural springs, salt flats, and mountain peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate valley walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Ollo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 450 hikers have used komoot to explore Ollo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ollo?
A: Ollo offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails. These routes vary in difficulty, exploring the region's valleys, natural springs, and mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Ollo?
A: Hiking in Ollo, located in the Valle de Ollo in Navarra, Spain, means exploring diverse landscapes. You'll encounter serene valleys, discover natural springs like the NacederoArteta, and pass through unique features such as the Arteta Salt Flats. Many trails also ascend to mountain peaks, offering varied elevation and scenic views.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Ollo?
A: Yes, Ollo has options for easier hikes. While many trails are moderate to difficult, there are at least two routes classified as easy. These typically offer gentler terrain, perfect for a relaxed outing or a family walk.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes with significant elevation gain in Ollo?
A: Absolutely. Ollo is known for its more challenging routes, with 13 trails classified as difficult. These often involve substantial elevation gain, leading to mountain peaks like Txurregi and Gaztelu, and providing rewarding summit views. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966183094/txurregi-and-gaztelu-loop-from-ollo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txurregi and Gaztelu Loop from Ollo&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant ascent.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls or natural springs?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural water features. You can explore trails that lead to the Artazul Waterfall or the NacederoArteta spring. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966024800/cascada-de-artazul-via-the-arteta-salt-flats-circular-from-ultzurrun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Artazul via the Arteta Salt Flats - circular from Ultzurrun&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past both the waterfall and the salt flats.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Ollo?
A: Many of the hiking routes in Ollo are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;NacederoArteta – Arteta Spring Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15002843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txurregi Summit (1,121 m) – Gaztelu loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can visit while hiking in Ollo?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Ollo offers several notable landmarks and viewpoints. You can hike to mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5136015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Lizarraga&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2043845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arteta (Alto de Goñi)&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider trails leading to summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txurregi Summit (1,121 m)&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3379347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beriain Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Ollo?
A: The trails in Ollo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, from tranquil valley walks to challenging mountain ascents, and the natural beauty of the springs and salt flats.

Q: Is it possible to hike to mountain peaks in the Ollo area?
A: Yes, the Ollo region provides excellent opportunities for peak bagging. Several trails lead to prominent summits, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txurregi Summit (1,121 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3379347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beriain Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through the Arteta Salt Flats?
A: Yes, the Arteta Salt Flats are a distinctive feature in the region and are accessible via hiking trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966024800/cascada-de-artazul-via-the-arteta-salt-flats-circular-from-ultzurrun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Artazul via the Arteta Salt Flats - circular from Ultzurrun&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that specifically includes this unique natural landmark.

Q: What is the average duration for a hike in Ollo?
A: Hike durations in Ollo vary significantly based on the trail's length and difficulty. Many popular routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966024800/cascada-de-artazul-via-the-arteta-salt-flats-circular-from-ultzurrun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Artazul via the Arteta Salt Flats&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in around 2 to 2.5 hours. More challenging mountain loops, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966183094/txurregi-and-gaztelu-loop-from-ollo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txurregi and Gaztelu Loop&lt;/a&gt;, may take 4 to 5 hours.
tips: The top of the Txurregi at 1121 meters above sea level is one of the classics in the Community of Navarra. It is ascended by paths with medium to high difficulty and you can choose one or the other as well as make a circular route or come and go along the same path. It is advisable to start from Ilzarbe or Ollo.

faqs.question: How long does the Txurregi from Ilzarbe – circular via Gaztelu hike typically take?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular hike is estimated to take around 4 hours and 20 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace, fitness level, and how long you spend enjoying the spectacular views from the summits.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, starting with grassy sections from Ilzarbe. You'll encounter stony areas, forested parts with oak and holm oak trees, and open pasture land. A significant feature is the very steep 'hard ramp' leading up to Txurregi, which requires appropriate footwear and potentially poles, especially if wet. The crest of Txurregi can be windy, and the initial descent from the summit is stony.

faqs.question: Is the Txurregi from Ilzarbe – circular via Gaztelu trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation gain, and the very steep 'hard ramp' to Txurregi, this trail is not recommended for beginners. It's best suited for hikers with a good level of fitness and experience. While some experienced hikers might find parts 'quite simple,' the overall challenge requires preparation.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Ilzarbe?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the village of Ilzarbe. While specific parking details aren't always explicit, you can typically find parking within or at the edge of small villages like Ilzarbe, often near the church or a central square. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlights are the summits themselves. You'll reach the Txurregi Summit (1,121 m) and Monte Gaztelu (998 m), both offering extensive panoramic views. On Monte Gaztelu, you can see the circular stone enclosure of an ancient watchtower. Both summits feature mailboxes typical of Spanish hiking peaks.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Txurregi from Ilzarbe – circular via Gaztelu trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, due to the challenging and steep sections, especially the 'hard ramp' to Txurregi, ensure your dog is fit enough for the ascent and descent. Always keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, to protect wildlife and for the safety of other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Txurregi from Ilzarbe – circular via Gaztelu trail. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this route are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making the steep ascents more challenging. Winter hikes are possible but require caution due to potential snow, ice, and slippery conditions on the steep sections, especially the 'hard ramp' to Txurregi.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the summits?
faqs.answer: From the peaks of Txurregi and Gaztelu, you can expect spectacular panoramic views. These include the Ollo Valley, Sakana, the Pamplona basin, and surrounding mountain ranges like Aralar, Andia, and Sarbil. On clear days, you might even see the Pyrenees to the east.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Tour de la Valle de Ollo and the Camino Txurregi.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, it's essential to bring sturdy hiking boots with good grip. Walking poles are highly recommended, especially for the steep 'hard ramp' and stony descents. Carry plenty of water, snacks, sun protection, and layers of clothing as the crest can be windy. A map or GPS device is also advisable.
